title: vaccinium vitis-idaea subsp. minus
intro: Vaccinium vitis-idaea subsp. minus, also known as dwarf bilberry or low-growing bilberry, is a sub-species of the bilberry plant that belongs to the heath family. It is a small, low-growing shrub native to the Arctic tundra and mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America, with edible fruits rich in antioxidants and medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Vaccinium vitis-idaea subsp. minus include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are known to help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and jams, which may help to promote overall health and wellbeing, as well as alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and other health conditions.
